Narrative_Meaning: Jacks signify youth, high spirits, parties, quarrels, protection, privilege and disagreements.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Mask card represents the false fronts that we play. There are few people who are completely open and honest with everyone in their life, most of us choose to hide aspects of ourselves from view and don a mask. For some, undercover agents and those running for their lives, the mask becomes their only existence.

Significator.Plot: The Mask Plot is a a plot about secret identities and double-lives. The Plot might be about Superheroes, dealing with a real life and a costumed identity, or an undercover cop in a crime syndicate, or a cyber-ninja infiltrating an opposed mega-Zaibatsu. Mask Plots can become farcical, unless there is real Tension that they will be discovered, and that it will have profound consequences. It is sometimes a necessary part of the Mask plot to have the hero discover exactly how bad it can get, with loved ones threatened or killed by the Plot.
Significator.Side: When a side is marked by the Mask the side may pretend to be something they are not, such as the Above pretending to be the Shadows or vice-versa.
Significator.Character:
The Mask Significator indicates a Character who can pretend to be things they are not.

They Gain Yin when they wear a mask, disguise or shape-shift.

They Gain Yang when they deceive another or pretend to be something they are not.

They Gain Chi when they are not recognised for who, or what, they are.

They also gain +1 Success Level with all disguise, masks and make-up actions.
Significator.Diegetic_Character: A master of deception and disguise, capable of assuming many faces and identities. They are at their best when hidden behind a mask, moving unseen and unrecognized through the world while expertly playing whatever role is required of them.
Significator.Scene: A Mask scene is a scene where things are not as they first appear. Perhaps the location is a Facade, such as movie set, or that what was intended to be paying in some cash to the bank, gets subverted when masked robbers walk in and unload a shotgun into the ceiling.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's Core. For example: In order to Hook a Traveller, create a barrier that stops them from travelling, or something that draws in Travellers like a roadside attraction of sorts.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Story opens with the Plot presenting a false face, it may be a pretext for something else, or a pretence of some sort, but the plot does not represent itself truthfully. It may hide its Conflict Embodiments (perhaps making the Player Characters not the Pressed Characters), make false Revelations, or false promises in general.
Yarn.Gain: Secret Identity (this can just be a disguise Descendant, or a whole lifestyle including Locations, or even a whole new Alternate)
Yarn.Diegetic_Gain: The creation of a perfect cover—a secret identity that provides a new life, a safe haven, and the freedom to act without being recognized.
Yarn.Fray_Description: Masquerades can be simple fancy dress parties, or they can be complex political or covert Ordeals full of under-the-table deals, or thinking bigger they can be their own Stories with sub-rosa themes, Quests to deceive, mislead and be underhanded with others, or even a Witness Protection Program or Deep Cover / Sleeper Agent situations that last decades.
Yarn.Snag_Description: There is some false information around, it may have already have misled the PCs, but worse yet the next Revelation they make will be deliberate misinformation by the Yarn-Teller or Plot. Perhaps they will be told the truth in the end, or might even spot the lie eventually, but this isn't a simple lie, it will be close to the truth and they will find supporting evidence.

NPC.Description: The Spy lives quietly, hiding amongst their enemies, they are a Pretender who will become whatever they need to be to avoid suspicion, while constantly working on their devious, deviant, plans. During active missions Spies will often become Leaders, or Scouts to maximise the gains they make.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: No matter the Fray the PCs get to vanish in the wind after it. They change or adopt disguises, head to a safe-house, or vanish into the night. They can rest, recover, shop, create or train for the next Fray.
Yarn.History_Description: The Mask indicates that the past of this situation has been changed by time and rumour, or altered deliberately, aspects of the true history have been rewritten, with cover stories being created. This may have been to protect some identities (or alliances), preserve some societal myth, or simply be by the mists of time.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Mask indicates that the current situation is based around disguises, masks and perceptions. Perhaps an enemy is disguised among you, a spy or a mole in your organisation, or more often you must don a disguise in order pass undiscovered through enemy territory or spy upon them. Disguises can involve playing a role, pretending to be something you are not, or just not being recognised for who you really are.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Mask indicates that the solution to your current situation is to bluff it out, this may be a simple lie, or a complex deception, reliant on many players, trickery and shenanigans.

Revelations.About_Description: The information is about some mystery or unknown thing. This might be a secret hidden down the ages, or just who took the last doughnut.
Revelations.Info_Description: You can find out a lot about a person from the company they keep. And it always helps to know how many are in the gang you are hunting.
Revelations.Vector_Description: Sometimes information includes something so preposterous that it is an obvious lie. Sometimes the obvious lie can hide a deeper truth, this can happen often when someone reveals they know nothing about a subject they have claimed knowledge of by asking a question they should obviously know the answer too. Worse yet is when the obvious lie is actually the truth, but made to seem so preposterous that it is dismissed out of hand.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The information is about how a character or descendant likes to be perceived. Sometimes this can be an alternate name, an alias, codename, pen name, or secret identity that they use. For example, traditionally Robin Hood is known to have employed a disguise as a peasant farmer at tournament.
Revelations.Detail_Description: Disguised details are those cleverly arranged details that misdirect and misinform the unwary observer, this can be as simple as making handwriting unrecognisable by writing with their off-hand, or as complex as arranging the sniper shot to exactly coincide with the cannon-fire in the 1812 Overture.

Obstacle.Description: The Obstacle requires you to Lie for all you're worth and pretend to be something you are not. It might be that the way ahead is only allowed for someone of a particular rank, gender, religion or race, and you have to bluff your way past Guardians.

Tarot.Normal: Grants Target an additional pile where cards may be played upon them.
Tarot.Crossed:
Closes Pile. All cards beneath the Double Bluff are discarded,
 no card may be played on the Double Bluff until a Crisis removes it.

Ordeal.Stage_Description: Jump down, Jump into shadows, a Joke, a Quantum Leap.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: Swim for the goal, before anyone else, to secure the victory.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: At least one of the Flowing Side that was previously defeated returns to the Battle with their Wounds Stabilised.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
A single Monster (usually with at least Two ♦ Monster Facets), that is at least equivalent to any 2 PCs.
  Facet    Monster  
Enigma ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Skulker — The Skulker is a shadowy figure often pulling strings behind the scenes. This may just be an ordinary villain, but their ability to pass unnoticed should be bordering upon the unnatural. Perhaps they use psychic powers, or magic, to cloud the minds of witnesses, then again perhaps they are a hacking whiz, able to bounce phone calls through international relays with some multinational picking up the tab without realizing, or perhaps they are a Cryptid, an Alien Big Cat, Wolf man, Sasquatch or one of the Huldra, the hidden people - who maybe Fæ or the imaginary friend of one of the characters, possibly made real(ish) by an alien or an Increated.
Skulkers start with a Monster Pool of Enigma Score. Skulkers may add Enigma Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they hide, and if they hide completely for a Chi Extend Duration equal to the Enigma Boon they may add Enigma Score cards to their Monster Pool (e.g. Enigma Boon 12 grants 1 card when they hide, but if the Skulker hides until Dawn / Dusk / Noon or Midnight they may add 4 cards to the Pool).
Skulkers must Discard or Play half their Monster Pool any time they are spotted or found out. Often this will be spent on an immediate Hide action. They may add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any stealthy, or hidden, actions or preparations. Skulkers that are hidden may play Monster Pool cards as Preparation to another’s actions as though they were making them. E.g. The Hacker can prepare a Hack attack, that is performed by a Church Website Server, in another town. A mind-bending villain could prepare a Psych Defence in the mind of his puppet. A Sasquatch might Prepare a Flee action on the hunter they have spotted in their territory, that they will trigger with a Fear emotional effect.
Heresy ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Masker — The Monster is looks entirely like something else. Perhaps they are a Demon in the form of an innocent child, or maybe they are a shape-shifter or face-dancer, able to change their appearance as they like, then again perhaps they’re just the old Janitor in a monster costume. The Masker may add Heresy Score cards to their Monster Pool whenever they change or don their disguise (or mask) and Heresy Draw cards to the Monster Pool whenever their disguise works, or they are believed in their lies. The Monster Pool adds its Size Reduced Success Levels to any Action to do with hiding, maintaining the mask, or lying. The Masker must play at least one card from their Monster Pool any time their disguise is seen, examined, or observed as part of the action.
Jeer ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Mockery — The monster is a mockery — It might be illusionary, or Undead (usually a corpse animated by a low-level Increated), but it may be a ghost, a machine or even an animal that mocks something else. The Mockery is created with a Monster Pool of Jeer Score cards, that must be played to maintain the Mockery (when disbelieved, attacked, etc). While the Monster Pool exists the Monster can pull and play Monster Pool Size Reduced cards (initially Jeer Draw) for any action the Mockery form takes that it has no suitable Annexes for. The Mockery may add Jeer Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they are mistaken for what they mock. E.g. A Skeleton Mockery might have a "Mortality Reminder" Annex, but is given a Monster Pool of 5, so it can initially pull and play 2 cards for its mocking actions (like moving around and clawing the living). However, once attacked (or disbelieved) the Skeleton must spend at least one card from the Mockery Pool, thereafter it can only draw and Play 1 card for any Action that doesn’t involve reminding someone of their own Mortality. If the Skeleton is mistaken for a living being, then it may add 1 card to its Mockery Monster Pool.
Liberty ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Nightmare — The Monster is nightmarish, or dreamlike. Such Monsters either exist in a purely psychological realm, appearing as dreams and hallucinations, that cannot really be fought and don’t physically exist, or they are extremely dangerous and powerful Monsters that really exist and may be magical or supernatural in power. Nightmares may add Liberty Draw cards to their Monster Pool if they cause a Psych wound or appear in a dream. While they have cards in their Monster Pool they may add Liberty Score (usually rolled) to everything they do. The Nightmare may spend at least one card from their Monster Pool to do something physically impossible, they might use it to fly through the air, instantly teleport, let bullets pass through themselves, discard physical wounds, or appear in the waking world as examples. A Nightmare must discard a Monster Pool Card if they cross a threshold, barrier, or liminal space or time, or take a Psych Wound. Flower-faced Humanoids and Canines from a nightmare realm, handsome soap-sellers with an odd idea for gentlemen’s clubs, facially-burned, gardening-gloved janitors turned serial killers, six foot tall invisible rabbits, night hags, shadow beings seen when falling asleep, and any character in a dream, are all good examples of the Liberty Monster.
Nature ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Elemental — The Monster is an elemental force of nature, perhaps a literal elemental or more likely metaphorical. The Elemental will add Nature Draw cards to its Monster Pool when it encounters a natural source of its element, or when it causes collateral damage to a new location. Elementals gain +1 Success Level per Monster Pool Size Reduced and +1 Score or Pips per Monster Pool card, to any and all actions to do with their natural element. The Elemental must spend at least 1 card from its Monster Pool when soaking attacks that are not based upon its element. Salamanders, Sylphs, Gnomes, and Undines, Giants (of most types), are all example Elementals, as well as Forces-of-Nature and natural disasters such as storms as well as Tsunamis, landslides, pyroclastic flows and even blizzards (most of which might be Giants anyway).
Phoenix ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Paragon — The Monster is the embodiment of a positive force or emotion, such as Love, Forgiveness, Warmth, Satisfaction or Wonder. Paragon Monsters are usually capable of causing positive (and sometimes negative) emotions via a Psych Attack in those that see them, and while they may be positive in nature, they are not necessarily good, or altruistic in their actions. The Paragon begins with a Monster Pool of cards equal to the Phoenix Score. The Paragon can discard half of their Monster Pool to multiply any Score by their Phoenix Score (usually rolled). The Paragon’s Monster Pool adds Phoenix Draw cards if the Paragon can find a source of their chosen emotion, and adds Phoenix Score cards if it can consume, interrupt or destroy this source (stopping a Character from feeling that emotion ever again would count as destroying the source, but so would eating them, changing their mood so they no longer feel that way woul be interrupting, as they could change back later). The Paragon must spend at least one card from its Monster Pool creating a Psych Attack for all observers whenever it is observed, which may be positive or Negative as the Monster / Yarn-Teller decides. The Phoenix itself is the embodiment of Resurrection, Hope and Faith, but is quite capable of burning destruction. The Golden Goose is another example, the embodiment of charity, but it evokes profound avarice in those who would own it. The unicorn is a symbol of purity, but is dangerous in the extreme to those it considers impure. Depictions of Angels are also perfect examples of positive embodiments.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Example: Human / Groonians / Eelafin / Eusaivean / Mer-person / Bigfoot / Giant / Goblin, etc
Lea.Notes: Broccen may use Jack’s to dig new tunnels in the Lea, creating species to fill niches. Jacks may also be used to create Chimerical changes, blending parts of different animals together, each Jack adds another place the Bulmäs can play Lea cards.

Stress.Description: Stress can have profound effects on the mind, causing one to see and hear all sorts of things that are not happening. Simply missing too much sleep can cause a healthy mind to micro-sleep and confuse the Character’s real and dream lives, and for some minds the confusion is more easily started, unusual brain chemistry and even normal development can lead to all manner of hallucinatory voices and imaginary friends knocking about the place.
Stress.Rules: The Character’s current Stress and the current Tension Level are added together to create an amount of Chi. This Chi must be paid by the Character to side-step the Character partially into a Psycho-social "dream-space" or "metaphorical representation" that they will explore, encountering aspects of their own Character, and perhaps their mental image of close friends as metaphors and hallucinatory Extras. The Delirium continues for a period of time equal to the Current Stress as Intrepid Facet Sway after which the delirium will fade away removing Stress equal to the current Tension Level. Typically the Delirium will reappear anytime the Character’s current Stress plus the Tension Level is high enough to side-step them that far again. Deliriums are typically an extension of the Character, even if they do appear as faery forests, cloud castles, creepy dungeons or alien vistas, so trying to harm them, or the character’s within with magic, for example, will usually harm the Character experiencing the delirium.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can alter Characters dramatically, but it can also cause Characters to pretend it has affected them differently than it actually has. Some people enjoy the attention that comes with trauma, and will exaggerate the effects for more attention. For others, pretending to be someone without the trauma is the game, if they pretend hard enough perhaps the trauma will go away. In some other cases, the Character may assume an unrelated symptom is part of the same thing, which can become incorporated into their diagnosis.
Trauma.Rules: The Character decides to mask the trauma in some way. Typically the mask will pretend the effects of the trauma were different to the actual effects in some way, often either more or less profound than they actually were. Characters can create an Alt that will act as the "mask" Character, or they can create a "Masking Behaviour" Annex or Descendant to help them hide what is happening (this will relieve Stress to Chi to purchase the Alt, Descendant or Annex).

Age.Description: An Age of Pretence is occurring. During this age the society values lying and pretence more than actual accomplishments. During this age the leaders of the society will also be liars and deceivers, and the society will usually be misled by them, for their own personal goals. The society will treat gossip, stories and anecdotes with equal validity often without checking for evidence.
Age.Diegetic_Description: A time of facades and falsehoods, where society values the appearance of things more than their reality. It is an era of deception and misinformation, where the truth is obscured by a web of rumors and the leaders themselves are masters of the lie.
Age.Rules: During the Age of Pretence clearly showing disbelief will be viewed as rude, insulting and have a negative impression within the society. Appearing to believe something that is probably false will be viewed positively within this society at this time.
